name: webhooks
context: Handle Stripe webhook events: verify signatures, process payment and subscription events
info: Stripe sends webhook events to your server when things happen in your account — payments succeed, subscriptions change, disputes are opened, and more. This skill covers setting up a webhook endpoint, verifying event signatures to prevent spoofing, processing common event types for payments and subscriptions, handling retries and failures, and best practices for idempotent event processing. Webhook signature verification uses the STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET (whsec_...) from the Stripe dashboard or CLI.

sections.title: Webhook endpoint setup
sections.body: Create a POST endpoint that receives raw JSON bodies. Register the URL in the Stripe dashboard (Developers > Webhooks) or use the Stripe CLI for local testing (`stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3000/webhook`). The endpoint must return 2xx within 20 seconds. Use `express.raw({ type: 'application/json' })` for Express — do not parse JSON before signature verification.

sections.title: Signature verification
sections.body: Call `stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(rawBody, signatureHeader, webhookSecret)` to verify and parse the event. This prevents spoofed events from attackers. The `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` (`whsec_...`) comes from the Stripe dashboard or CLI. If verification fails, return 400. Never process events without verification in production.

sections.title: Common event types
sections.body: Payment events: `payment_intent.succeeded`, `payment_intent.payment_failed` (see [[stripe/payments]]). Checkout events: `checkout.session.completed`, `checkout.session.expired` (see [[stripe/checkout#fulfillment]]). Subscription events: `customer.subscription.created`, `customer.subscription.updated`, `customer.subscription.deleted`, `customer.subscription.trial_will_end` (see [[stripe/subscriptions]]). Invoice events: `invoice.payment_failed`, `invoice.paid`. Dispute: `charge.dispute.created`.

sections.title: Retry behavior
sections.body: Stripe retries failed deliveries (non-2xx responses) with exponential backoff for up to 3 days. Endpoints must respond within 20 seconds. For slow processing, acknowledge immediately (return 200) and process asynchronously via a queue. Events may arrive out of order — always fetch the current state from the API if order matters.

sections.title: Best practices
sections.body: Always verify signatures. Make handlers idempotent using `event.id` as a deduplication key. Return 200 quickly, process asynchronously. Handle events you care about, ignore the rest (return 200 for unknown types). Use the Stripe CLI for local development. In production, register specific event types rather than receiving all events. Monitor webhook delivery in the Stripe dashboard.

scripts.description: Set up an Express endpoint to receive and verify Stripe webhook events

STRIPE_SECRET_KEY.description: Stripe secret API key (sk_test_... or sk_live_...)

payload.description: Raw request body as string or Buffer

data.description: Event data object containing the affected resource
